seoTitle: Fix Broken Excel Links by Changing Data Source Path
metaDescription: Learn how to change the Excel data source path in Power BI to fix broken links after a file gets moved, renamed, or shared to a new location.
intro: Reports built on Excel data break the moment the underlying file moves, gets renamed, or is shared to a different folder path, and Power BI won't fix that on its own. This guide covers locating the current broken link and updating the data source path so your report reconnects properly. It's a short fix aimed at anyone who's opened a report to find it can't find its Excel file anymore.

steps.heading: Understand why the data source path breaks
steps.body: Excel-based data sources in Power BI reference an exact file path, so moving, renaming, or relocating the file to a shared drive will break the connection.

steps.heading: Locate the broken link
steps.body: Open the Power BI report and check for a data source error, then go to Data Source Settings to see which Excel connection is failing.

steps.heading: Change the data source path
steps.body: In Data Source Settings, select the affected source and use the Change Source option to point it to the file's current location.

steps.heading: Ensure data consistency going forward
steps.body: Consider storing the Excel file in a stable, shared location like OneDrive or SharePoint so future moves are less likely to break the connection.

faq.question: Where do I fix a broken Excel data source in Power BI?
faq.answer: Go to File > Options and Settings > Data Source Settings, select the broken connection, and use Change Source to update the file path.

faq.question: Why does my Power BI report break every time I move the Excel file?
faq.answer: This happens because Power BI stores the exact file path used when the connection was first created, so any change to that path requires manually updating the source.

faq.question: How can I prevent broken links to Excel files in the future?
faq.answer: Storing your Excel source in a consistent shared location, like a OneDrive or SharePoint folder rather than a local drive, reduces the chances of the path changing unexpectedly.

faq.question: Does fixing the data source path affect my existing report visuals?
faq.answer: No, updating the path just reconnects Power BI to the correct file — as long as the structure of the data hasn't changed, your visuals and measures stay intact.
